feed.title: SAP Community - ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
feed.subtitle: ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP RAP Events local Consumption
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am seeing a weird issue with local event consumption of standard RAP events. So, I have seen if we create Purchase order from ME21N SAP GUI, standard RAP BO event Created of R_PurchaseOrderTP is triggered. Once, I implemented the local handler for this entity event, it doesn't trigger immediately. But once I go inside debugging mode, it starts triggering. 

 

Have anyone else faced this issue? Do we have to activate or run any program for this?

entry.title: SAP RAP: Automatically add row to table when another entity is created.
entry.content:
Hello,
I have a question regarding SAP RAP development.

I have a case like: one root entity and two children entities A and B with cardinality [0..*]. I use managed RAP scenario with draft and I want to create automatically entity B in draft when the entity A in draft is created. Is it possible somehow?
So it should look like: I have object page of root entity, there are two tables of entities A and B. When I click create on the table with entity A - row to the table with entity B should also be added.
How to achieve that?

Thanks in advance,
Mikolaj

entry.title: Display Icon in object header
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying t upload an icon on object page header in RAP application.
I am providing values form SAP-icons for example sap-icon://accelerated. it is populating the error "Annotation value must not contain the // character" . Could you please help me to understand how to pass correct value to image URL 

Thanks

entry.title: I want to use T table example T095 in rap
entry.content:
if_rap_query_provider~select

SELECT * FROM T095 into table @DATA(lt_t095).

Hello expert, I would like to use the T table, but it indicates that it is not allowed. Is there any other alternative method

entry.title: Determination triggering fields - ABAP RESTful Programming Model (RAP) - Managed scenario
entry.content:
How to Identify Which Field Triggered a Determination?

Let's assume we have defined a determination on modify that triggers when two fields are modified.
An example would be:

determination calculateTotalPrice on modify { update; field BookingFee, CurrencyCode; }

The method FOR DETERMINE ON MODIFY that implements the determination has only the keys of the entities linked to the determination as input.

How can I determine whether the modification was triggered by changes to the BookingFee field or the CurrencyCode field?

 
ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model

entry.title: Issue in UNIT_CONVERSION in CDS View Entity
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to perform unit conversion in CDS view entities in version 75I. So, trying to use unit_conversion, however it ends with SQL code 719.
My CDS view:

Error with execution:

Database returned the SQL code 719. Error text: currency/unit conversion error: search table error: Conversion( "COL$11$" ),
row 0: could not determine valid conversion rate for value(s) 7 from unit 'ST' to unit 'KAR' for client '010'

Both these unit are part of T006 table.

Could you please provide valuable inputs to solve the same.

Regards,

JR

entry.title: Get technical information from Service Definition using ABAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

Does anyone know if there is any class or function module I could use to read the technical information of a service definition? For example, I want to get the entities exposed from the service definition along with their alias and then also read the fields of these entities with their alias. If there is no such class or function module, does anyone know on which tables I can find these information?

Thank you!

entry.title: OData response time increased significantly after model changes and reduces after 8-9 hours
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing a strange issue.

I have built an unmanaged RAP application with 8 entities in SAP on-prem system where all of them are related to each other via associations and compositions. Each entity on an average are having around 25-30 fields. I have created cds, behavior, metadata extensions, service definition and OData V2 UI service binding.

All works good, but the issue is, whenever I change any thing in my model like field logic, or add additional field etc., OData response slows down significantly.

It takes around 35 - 40 seconds to load metadata sometimes it takes even minutes. It again fastens up after 8 - 9 hours and loads in 600 - 800ms.

I tried doing everything including

invalidating global cache

invalidating client cache

invalidating metadata cache

I even tried running report /UI5/UPD_ODATA_METADATA_CACHE

Nothing helps

I would be happy to know what is the potential cause for this and how to resolve

 

metadata loads in 670ms without any modifications.

 

Same service taking 35 seconds after field modification

entry.title: 'IAM APP' does not appear in eclipse
entry.content:
Hi guys

In eclipse I right click on the package and select New > Other Repository Object.

I try to create an 'IAM app' but it doesn't appear as if it doesn't exist:

I tried reinstalling the ABAP tools but the error persists

entry.title: RAP on Dynamic Condition Tables – Draft Enablement Challenge
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m working on a requirement where the underlying tables are dynamic (condition tables). I need to build a RAP application on top of this setup. However, I'm facing a challenge: I can't enable draft functionality because the CDS view is a query implemented by a class, and draft is not supported in this scenario.

Draft functionality is crucial for my use case. Does anyone have experience with a similar setup or suggestions on how to handle this? Any ideas on enabling draft or dynamically handling such data in a RAP application would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: bapi_po_create1 : passing standard table field J_1BNBM into bapi
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,
I have a field J_1BNBM in EKPO table which is getting field with control code.
There is validaion in po creation ME21N using method IF_EX_ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST~PROCESS_ITEM to make the field as mandatory due to which I am getting the
same error while using the BAPI_PO_CREATE1.
However, there is no field available  in line item of BAPI_PO_CREATE1 of related to J_1BNBM or control code.
The bapi will only post the PO if I could able to pass control_code(J_1BNBM)  using the same BAPI.
I thought of using as well as tried to achieve it through extension . But extension is for only customer add on fields .

J_1BNBM is standard field in EKPO and not customer field.

How to resolve this issue as I am at crossroads now ?

entry.title: How to call static action in controller
entry.content:
I have implemented a static action in bdef using RAP.

This action contains deep input parameter.

In the fiori custom controller, I am trying to call the action using "invokeaction".

I want to pass parameters of extracted data from an csv.

But since action is bound, it requires a context. I tried ways to create a context but the invoke action always give me these errors:

  •  EditFlow.invokeAction:Error: Pass a context for a bound action
  • Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getMetaPath')

need your advice on how to call an action while passing a deep parameter.

is there way to make the action entity-unbound?

Note: service binding I use is V4 UI.

entry.title: Error Create Basic FreeStyle VSCode
entry.content:
Hi!

  After completing the Trial Period of my BTP Trail, I renewed it as I always did.
  However, this last time, after creating my CDS ABAP RAP through Eclipse, I tried to create SAP Fiori Generator (Basic FreeStyle) through VSCode and it shows this error in "Data Source: Connect to a System" and "System: yyyy@xxx.com". The error is attached. Could you help me?

 

Thank you!

entry.title: RAP / Restful Application Programming Model: Generate a PopUp prior to performing the Create-Method
entry.content:
Hi all,

in the context of ABAP RAP in an unmanaged scenario, BDEF, BIMP and appropiate CDS-Views (mostly compositions) have been defined. OData V2 is used in context of the unmanaged RAP BO.

On the initial list-report-page, there exists the CRUD-create-button. If the button is pressed, the RAP-create-method runs. I would like to implement the app such that:
1. First a pop-up is generated with some mandatory fields
2. after the mandatory fields have been entered and the pop-up is confirmed, that only then the actual CRUD-create-method is run.

So in essence, the process should be:
1. Press Button
2. PopUp generates
3. PopUp is filled by user and confirmed (=> This should automatically start the CRUD-create)
4. Some logic is performed, fields (for example FirstName and LastName) from the pop-up are taken, some extra logic is performed to get some additional fields and data from some tables and the results should then be filled into the corresponding fields when entering the CRUD-create-method
5. CRUD-create-method is automatically run after the pop-up had been confirmed, inside the UI the fields FirstName and LastName are automatically filled (possibly read-only now) and some more fields are additionally filled based on FirstName and LastName (for example, Street)

The idea is to have some necessary fields be filled in the PopUp which then directly pre-fill some more fields when entering the CRUD-create-method. Can that be implemented purely in RAP?

Thanks in advance for every answer and for taking the time!

entry.title: Unit test is diffrence between Cloud and on-premise
entry.content:
Dear experts, when I was testing Unit test in the local version of the system, I found that after using the commit entities statement, the data was persistently stored in the DB, and the ROLLBACK ENTITIES statement did not take effect. May I ask if this is a BUG? The following is my test code. The problem occurs in the 'check_input_is_validate' method:

"!@testing BDEF:ZI_UT_ROOT
CLASS ltc_ZI_UT_ROOT
DEFINITION FINAL FOR TESTING
DURATION SHORT
RISK LEVEL HARMLESS.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA:cds_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_cds_test_environment,
               sql_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_osql_test_environment.

    CLASS-METHODS:
      "! Instantiate class under test and setup test double frameworks
      class_setup,

      "! Destroy test environments and test doubles
      class_teardown.

    METHODS:
      "! Reset test doubles
      setup,

      "! Reset transactional buffer
      teardown,

      "! Check input is validate
      check_input_is_validate FOR TESTING,

      "! Check input is invalid
      check_input_is_invalid FOR TESTING,

      "! Check returned features for instance with overall status 'Open'
      check_setstatustoopen      FOR TESTING,

      "! Check returned features for instance with overall status 'Rejected'
      get_features_rejected      FOR TESTING,

      "! Check action result is right
      check_action_result FOR TESTING.

ENDCLASS.


CLASS ltc_ZI_UT_ROOT IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD class_setup.
    cds_test_environment = cl_cds_test_environment=>create( i_for_entity = 'ZI_UT_ROOT' ).
    sql_test_environment = cl_osql_test_environment=>create( i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'I_SUPPLIER_VH' ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD class_teardown.
    cds_test_environment->destroy( ).
    sql_test_environment->destroy( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_setstatustoopen.
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        CREATE FIELDS ( Status )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %cid   = 'Root'
                        Status = ''  ) )
        MAPPED DATA(mapped)
        FAILED DATA(faild)
        REPORTED DATA(reported).

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        ALL FIELDS WITH
        CORRESPONDING #( mapped-zi_ut_root )
        RESULT DATA(root_results).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( msg = 'Status should be O!' exp = 'O' act = root_results[ 1 ]-Status ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_features_rejected.
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        CREATE FIELDS ( Status )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %cid   = 'Root'
                        Status = 'R'  ) )
        MAPPED DATA(mapped)
        FAILED DATA(faild)
        REPORTED DATA(reported).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
            EXECUTE rejectOrder
            FROM VALUE #( ( %tky = mapped-zi_ut_root[ 1 ]-%tky  ) )
        FAILED DATA(failed_action)
        REPORTED DATA(reported_action).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = lines( failed_action-zi_ut_root ) exp = 1 msg = 'Should has error' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = failed_action-zi_ut_root[ 1 ]-Orderid ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( exp = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                        act = failed_action-zi_ut_root[ 1 ]-%action-rejectOrder
                                        msg = 'rejectOrder action should be disabled').
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD setup.
    cds_test_environment->clear_doubles( ).
    sql_test_environment->clear_doubles( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_action_result.
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        CREATE FIELDS ( TotalPrice )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %cid   = 'Root'
                        TotalPrice = '100'  ) )
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
            EXECUTE deductDiscount
            FROM VALUE #( ( %cid_ref = 'Root'
                            %param-discount_percent = '10' ) )
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
            EXECUTE acceptOrder
            FROM VALUE #( ( %cid_ref = 'Root' ) )
        MAPPED DATA(mapped)
        FAILED DATA(faild)
        REPORTED DATA(reported).

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
       ENTITY zi_ut_root
       ALL FIELDS WITH
       CORRESPONDING #( mapped-zi_ut_root )
       RESULT DATA(root_results).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = lines( root_results ) exp = 1 msg = 'Should has result' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = root_results[ 1 ]-TotalPrice exp = '90' msg = 'Total price should be 90' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = root_results[ 1 ]-Status exp = 'A' msg = 'Status should be A' ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD teardown.
    ROLLBACK ENTITIES.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_input_is_validate.
    DATA vendor_mock_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i_supplier_vh.

    vendor_mock_data = VALUE #( ( Supplier = 'test' ) ).
    sql_test_environment->insert_test_data( vendor_mock_data ).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        CREATE FIELDS ( Status vendor BeginDate EndDate )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %cid   = 'Root'
                        Status = ''
                        vendor = 'test'
                        BeginDate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) - 10
                        EndDate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) + 10 ) )
    MAPPED DATA(mapped)
    FAILED DATA(faild)
    REPORTED DATA(reported).

*    COMMIT ENTITIES IN SIMULATION MODE RESPONSES
    COMMIT ENTITIES RESPONSES
        FAILED DATA(commit_failed)
        REPORTED DATA(commit_reported).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_initial( act = commit_failed msg = `Should't has error` ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_input_is_invalid.
    DATA vendor_mock_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i_supplier_vh.

    vendor_mock_data = VALUE #( ( Supplier = 'test' ) ).
    sql_test_environment->insert_test_data( vendor_mock_data ).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        CREATE FIELDS ( Status vendor BeginDate EndDate )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %cid   = 'Root'
                        Status = 'X'
                        vendor = 'test_1'
                        BeginDate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) + 10
                        EndDate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) - 10 ) )
    MAPPED DATA(mapped)
    FAILED DATA(faild)
    REPORTED DATA(reported).

    COMMIT ENTITIES IN SIMULATION MODE RESPONSE OF zi_ut_root
        FAILED DATA(commit_failed)
        REPORTED DATA(commit_reported).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = commit_reported-zi_ut_root[ %element-status = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ]
                                             msg = 'Status should be marked as error' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = commit_reported-zi_ut_root[ %element-vendor = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ]
                                             msg = 'Vendor should be marked as error' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = commit_reported-zi_ut_root[ %element-BeginDate = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ]
                                             msg = 'BeginDate should be marked as error' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = commit_reported-zi_ut_root[ %element-EndDate = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ]
                                             msg = 'EndDate should be marked as error' ).
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

 BDEF Code：

managed implementation in class zbp_i_ut_root unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for zi_ut_root //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zrap_ut_root
draft table zrap_ut_root_df
lock master total etag LastChangedAt
authorization master ( instance )
early numbering
//etag master <field_name>
{
  create;
  update ( features : instance );
  delete ( features : instance );

  draft action ( features : instance ) Edit;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare
  {
    validation checkInput;
  }

  determination setStatusToOpen on modify { create; update; }

  validation checkInput on save { create; update; field Status, Vendor, BeginDate, EndDate; }

  action ( features : instance ) deductDiscount parameter zp_ut_root_discount result [1] $self;
  action ( features : instance ) acceptOrder;
  action ( features : instance ) rejectOrder;

  side effects
  {
    action acceptOrder affects $self;
    action rejectOrder affects $self;
  }

  field ( readonly )
  Orderid,
  CreatedBy,
  CreatedAt,
  LocalLastChangedBy,
  LocalLastChangedAt,
  LastChangedAt;

  field ( mandatory )
  Status,
  Vendor,
  BeginDate,
  EndDate;

  association _item { create; with draft; }

  mapping for zrap_ut_root control zrap_ut_root_ct
    {
      Orderid            = orderid;
      Status             = status;
      Vendor             = vendor;
      BeginDate          = begin_date;
      EndDate            = end_date;
      TotalPrice         = total_price;
      CurrencyCode       = currency_code;
      CreatedBy          = created_by;
      CreatedAt          = created_at;
      LocalLastChangedBy = local_last_changed_by;
      LocalLastChangedAt = local_last_changed_at;
      LastChangedAt      = last_changed_at;
    }
}

define behavior for zi_ut_item //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zrap_ut_item
draft table zrap_ut_item_df
lock dependent by _root
authorization dependent by _root
early numbering
//etag master <field_name>
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly )
  Orderid,
  Itemid,
  CreatedBy,
  CreatedAt,
  LocalLastChangedBy,
  LocalLastChangedAt,
  LastChangedAt;

  association _root { with draft; }
  association _subitm { create; with draft; }

  mapping for zrap_ut_item control zrap_ut_item_ct
    {
      Orderid            = orderid;
      Itemid             = itemid;
      Component          = component;
      Quantity           = quantity;
      Unit               = unit;
      CreatedBy          = created_by;
      CreatedAt          = created_at;
      LocalLastChangedBy = local_last_changed_by;
      LocalLastChangedAt = local_last_changed_at;
      LastChangedAt      = last_changed_at;
    }
}

define behavior for zi_ut_subitm //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zrap_ut_subitm
draft table zrap_ut_sub_df
lock dependent by _root
authorization dependent by _root
early numbering
//etag master <field_name>
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly )
  Orderid,
  Itemid,
  Subitemid,
  CreatedBy,
  CreatedAt,
  LocalLastChangedBy,
  LocalLastChangedAt,
  LastChangedAt;

  association _root { with draft; }
  association _item { with draft; }

  mapping for zrap_ut_subitm control zrap_ut_subitm_ct
    {
      Orderid            = orderid;
      Itemid             = itemid;
      Subitemid          = subitemid;
      ScheduleDate       = schedule_date;
      Quantity           = quantity;
      Unit               = unit;
      Customer           = customer;
      CreatedBy          = created_by;
      CreatedAt          = created_at;
      LocalLastChangedBy = local_last_changed_by;
      LocalLastChangedAt = local_last_changed_at;
      LastChangedAt      = last_changed_at;
    }
}

Root entity code:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface for Unit test --Root view'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define root view entity zi_ut_root
  as select from zrap_ut_root
  composition [0..*] of zi_ut_item as _item
  association to I_Supplier_VH     as _supp on $projection.Vendor = _supp.Supplier
{
      @EndUserText.label: 'ID'
  key orderid               as Orderid,
      status                as Status,
      vendor                as Vendor,
      _supp.SupplierName    as VendorName,
      begin_date            as BeginDate,
      end_date              as EndDate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      total_price           as TotalPrice,
      currency_code         as CurrencyCode,
      @Semantics.user.createdBy: true
      created_by            as CreatedBy,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true
      created_at            as CreatedAt,
      @Semantics.user.lastChangedBy: true
      local_last_changed_by as LocalLastChangedBy,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
      local_last_changed_at as LocalLastChangedAt,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.lastChangedAt: true
      last_changed_at       as LastChangedAt,
      //    _association_name // Make association public

      _item,
      _supp
}

When I perform the unit test, this test data will be persistently stored IN the DB. I think this is incorrect unless I use COMMIT ENTITIES IN SIMULATION MODE RESPONSES instead of COMMIT ENTITIES RESPONSES，Can anyone help me solve this doubt? Thank you very much.

entry.title: How to add task to an existing transport request using program
entry.content:
PARAMETERS: p_TR TYPE trkorr OBLIGATORY. // Take TR as parameter

 

DATA: lt_authorlist TYPE trwbo_users,

lt_return TYPE e070-trkorr,

ls_return TYPE bapiret2,

lv_new_task TYPE trkorr. // Variable to take task

 

START-OF-SELECTION.

 

 

CALL FUNCTION 'TRINT_INSERT_NEW_COMM'

EXPORTING

wi_kurztext = 'New Task' // Description if creating TR

wi_trfunction = 'S' // 'S' to create task under TR

iv_username = sy-uname // System Username

wi_strkorr = p_TR // Export TR value to FM

IMPORTING

we_trkorr = lv_new_task // Import task to the variable

EXCEPTIONS

no_systemname = 1

no_systemtype = 2

no_authorization = 3

db_access_error = 4

file_access_error = 5

enqueue_error = 6

number_range_full = 7

invalid_input = 8

OTHERS = 9.

IF sy-subrc = 0.

WRITE: / ' New task created under TR:', p_TR,

/ ' Task number:', lv_new_task.

ELSE.

WRITE: / 'Failed to create task. '.

ENDIF.

*********************************************************************************
Type of request/task
Domain : TRFUNCTION
K : Workbench Request
W : Customizing Request
S : Development/Correction
Q : Customizing Task

entry.title: How to consume RAP BO Events of the Document Info Record (DIR) locally in SAP Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello everybody,

actually i am trying to consume a event (locally) of the document info record (DIR) in SAP Public Cloud embedded steampunk. In the Accelerator Hub there are some listed events for the DIR. However i cannot find the Business Object Interface for the DIR with the corresponding events. 

 

Does that mean that the events cannot be consumed at all altough they are listed and released?

 

 

Is there any way to consume this events locally in SAP Public Cloud?

entry.title: Enterprise Event Enablement - Creating custom event using RAP in S/4 OP
entry.content:
I am trying to capture events triggered on PM Maintenance Order and send them to SAP Event Mesh using the standard Enterprise Event Enablement framework on S/4HANA. It works for create and phase changes like 'In Planning', 'Released' and 'Complete'. However, it does not work for classic 'Change'.  In the standard BO: BUS2007 - Maintenance Order, there is no standard event topic for change of data, meaning, S/4 does not trigger events when something changes in the Maintenance Order. For example, I would like S/4 to fire an event when there is a change in the 'Basic Start Date' of the Maintenance Order. However, there is no standard event topic for 'change'. These are the below events available but they do not trigger when something changes in the Order. 

I have checked the RAP behavior definition for this BO : R_MAINTENANCEORDERTP and also in the list of events in the BOR for the BO: BUS2007, there is no appropriate event defined for change of data.

Is there a way in S/4 that I can extend the standard functionality to trigger an event when something changes on this BO? I want to bind this event to the standard Enterprise Event Enablement framework to send it to SAP Event Mesh. I have gone through a lot of documentation on RAP extensibility of business events but I am unable to wire them together because of my lack of experience in RAP development. 

Can someone show pointers on how this can be achieved?

#SAPEventMesh #SAPEnterpriseEventEnablement SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Error while processing building block SituationsIndicator in Custom Fiori app
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a custom Fiori app built using RAP, ODATA V4 UI

Project is my root entity.

I get this error in the Object Page's header section. 

Other sections are displayed correctly.

entry.title: How to Connect Eclipse with Git or abapGit and Push ABAP Code to GitHub for ABAP Cloud project
entry.content:
I'm working on an ABAP development project and would like to integrate version control using GitHub.

I’m specifically looking to push ABAP code to GitHub directly from Eclipse using either Git or the abapGit plugin.

What I've done so far:

  • Installed Egit plugin in Eclipse via the Marketplace.

  • Installed the abapGit plugin in Eclipse following the SAP Developers guide:
    https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-install-abapgit-plugin.html

I'm trying to understand:

  • Can I push ABAP code directly from Eclipse ADT to GitHub?

  • If yes, what are the exact steps to do this using either Git or abapGit in Eclipse?

  • If not, what is the recommended way to use GitHub for ABAP version control (especially in ABAP cloud  project)?
